ar.fadp.fadp_banner_body: Under the Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection, we need your explicit consent before using AI-powered features like smart matching and personalisation.
ar.hub.doc_accessibility_desc: Our commitment to making the platform accessible to everyone, including our progress toward WCAG 2.1 AA conformance.
ar.hub.doc_trust_safety_desc: Plain-English guide to how exchanges work, what we do to keep the community safe, what we don’t, and how to report a safeguarding concern.
ar.hub.platform_section_desc: These documents govern the Project NEXUS platform infrastructure and are separate from your community's policies.
ar.page_meta.trust_safety.description: How exchanges work on {{name}}, what we do to keep the community safe, what we don’t do, and how to report a concern. Plain-English trust and safeguarding.
ar.page_meta.trust_safety.title: Trust & Safety
ar.trust_safety.contact_cta_body: Get in touch and a coordinator will get back to you. Most messages are answered within one working day.
ar.trust_safety.contact_cta_title: Have a question we haven’t answered?
ar.trust_safety.disputes_intro: Most disagreements can be sorted out between members. If they can’t, we’ll help.
ar.trust_safety.disputes_steps.0: Try first to resolve it directly through messages — most misunderstandings are just that.
ar.trust_safety.disputes_steps.1: If that doesn’t work, contact a coordinator via the Contact page. Coordinators can mediate.
ar.trust_safety.disputes_steps.2: If a member breaches our Community Guidelines, we take action — warnings, mediation, suspension, or removal, depending on severity.
ar.trust_safety.disputes_steps.3: Time-credit disputes (hours logged in error, etc.) are reviewed by an admin who can adjust balances on either side.
ar.trust_safety.heading: Trust & Safety
ar.trust_safety.how_exchanges_intro: Every exchange follows the same simple flow. There is no money involved — just time credits.
ar.trust_safety.how_exchanges_steps.0: Find a member offering what you need, or post a request describing what you’re looking for.
ar.trust_safety.how_exchanges_steps.1: Message them through the platform to agree the details — time, place, and what’s involved.
ar.trust_safety.how_exchanges_steps.2: Complete the exchange. You only meet in person if you both want to.
ar.trust_safety.how_exchanges_steps.3: Log the hours. Both members confirm — that’s the receipt.
ar.trust_safety.how_exchanges_steps.4: Time credits transfer automatically. One hour of help = one time credit, no matter what skill was shared.
ar.trust_safety.insurance_items.0: {{name}} does not provide insurance for exchanges between members. The platform is a connection service — it does not insure, indemnify, or underwrite the work members do for each other.
ar.trust_safety.insurance_items.1: Members are NOT covered by any platform insurance for the work they do for each other. Exchanges are entirely between members and at members’ own risk. See our Terms of Service for the full liability and indemnity position.
ar.trust_safety.insurance_items.2: If you offer professional services (plumbing, electrical, healthcare, legal, financial advice), you’re responsible for your own professional indemnity insurance and any regulatory obligations that apply.
ar.trust_safety.insurance_items.3: Time credits have no monetary value. They can’t be exchanged for cash, sold, or used outside the timebanking community.
ar.trust_safety.meta_description: How exchanges work on {{name}}, what we do to keep the community safe, what we don’t do, and how to report a concern. Plain-English trust and safeguarding.
ar.trust_safety.precautions_intro: You’d apply the same common sense here as you would meeting anyone new through a community group.
ar.trust_safety.precautions_items.0: Meet in a public place the first time, or bring a friend if you’re inviting someone to your home.
ar.trust_safety.precautions_items.1: Read the other member’s reviews and check how long they’ve been on the platform.
ar.trust_safety.precautions_items.3: Trust your instincts. You can say no, end an exchange, or report a concern at any time.
ar.trust_safety.precautions_title: Sensible precautions for a first exchange
ar.trust_safety.responsibilities_items.0: Treat other members with respect (Community Guidelines).
ar.trust_safety.responsibilities_items.1: Be honest about your skills, qualifications, and limitations.
ar.trust_safety.responsibilities_items.2: Show up when you commit, or cancel with reasonable notice.
ar.trust_safety.responsibilities_items.3: Log hours fairly — for the time actually given.
ar.trust_safety.responsibilities_items.4: Use the platform only for community time exchanges, not paid work, sales solicitation, or anything illegal (Acceptable Use Policy).
ar.trust_safety.rights_items.0: Leave at any time and request your data be deleted (GDPR).
ar.trust_safety.rights_items.1: Block other members so they can’t contact you.
ar.trust_safety.rights_items.2: Make your profile invisible to search or browsing.
ar.trust_safety.rights_items.3: Opt out of all optional cookies, analytics, and email notifications.
ar.trust_safety.safeguarding_body: If you’re worried about a member, an exchange, or something you’ve seen on the platform, please tell us. We respond to safeguarding reports within one working day.
ar.trust_safety.safeguarding_step_1: Use the Report button on the relevant listing, message, or profile.
ar.trust_safety.safeguarding_step_2: Or email us via the Contact page — mark the subject as “Safeguarding”.
ar.trust_safety.subtitle: {{name}} connects neighbours so they can share skills using time credits. This page explains how that works in practice — what the platform does, what it doesn’t, and how you can stay safe.
ar.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.0: Verifies every member’s email address at registration; offers optional photo-ID verification with a Verified Member badge for those who want extra trust signals.
ar.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.1: Shows reviews and ratings on member profiles so the community can see who’s been a reliable exchange partner.
ar.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.2: Asks every member to accept our Community Guidelines and Acceptable Use Policy at signup, and to keep to them while they’re here.
ar.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.3: Gives every listing, message, and profile a Report button that goes straight to coordinators.
ar.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.4: Has human coordinators who can mediate, support, and step in when something goes wrong.
ar.trust_safety.what_we_dont_intro: Timebanking is built on neighbours helping neighbours, not on us standing between you. That’s a feature, not a bug — but it does mean we need to be clear about our limits.
ar.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.0: We don’t vet, train, or certify members for specific services. We’re not a recruitment agency.
ar.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.1: We don’t provide insurance coverage for the work members do for each other.
ar.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.2: We don’t supervise exchanges or act as anyone’s employer or service provider.
ar.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.3: We don’t guarantee any specific outcome, level of skill, or punctuality. Reviews are how the community keeps itself accountable.

fr.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.0: We don’t vet, train, or certify members for specific services. We’re not a recruitment agency.
fr.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.1: We don’t provide insurance coverage for the work members do for each other.
fr.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.2: We don’t supervise exchanges or act as anyone’s employer or service provider.
fr.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.3: We don’t guarantee any specific outcome, level of skill, or punctuality. Reviews are how the community keeps itself accountable.

ga.accessibility.meta_description: Our commitment to making the platform accessible to everyone, following WCAG 2.1 AA standards.
ga.cookies.meta_description: Information about how we use cookies to improve your experience on our platform.
ga.fadp.fadp_banner_body: Under the Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection, we need your explicit consent before using AI-powered features like smart matching and personalisation.
ga.hub.doc_accessibility_desc: Our commitment to making the platform accessible to everyone, including our progress toward WCAG 2.1 AA conformance.
ga.hub.doc_trust_safety_desc: Plain-English guide to how exchanges work, what we do to keep the community safe, what we don’t, and how to report a safeguarding concern.
ga.hub.platform_section_desc: These documents govern the Project NEXUS platform infrastructure and are separate from your community's policies.
ga.page_meta.acceptable_use.description: Acceptable use policy for our community timebanking platform.
ga.page_meta.acceptable_use.title: Acceptable Use Policy
ga.page_meta.community_guidelines.descripti: Community guidelines for respectful and productive timebanking.
ga.page_meta.community_guidelines.title: Community Guidelines
ga.page_meta.legal_hub.description: Legal documents, policies, and compliance information.
ga.page_meta.legal_hub.title: Legal Information
ga.page_meta.legal_version_history.descript: Version history of our legal documents and policies.
ga.page_meta.legal_version_history.title: Legal Version History
ga.page_meta.platform_disclaimer.descriptio: Important disclaimers about the use of the NEXUS community timebanking platform.
ga.page_meta.platform_disclaimer.title: Platform Disclaimer
ga.page_meta.platform_privacy.description: How the NEXUS platform collects, processes, and protects your personal data.
ga.page_meta.platform_privacy.title: Platform Privacy Policy
ga.page_meta.platform_terms.description: Terms of service governing use of the NEXUS community timebanking platform.
ga.page_meta.platform_terms.title: Platform Terms of Service
ga.page_meta.trust_safety.description: How exchanges work on {{name}}, what we do to keep the community safe, what we don’t do, and how to report a concern. Plain-English trust and safeguarding.
ga.page_meta.trust_safety.title: Trust & Safety
ga.privacy.meta_description: How we collect, use, and protect your personal data on our community platform.
ga.terms.meta_description: Terms and conditions governing the use of our community timebanking platform.
ga.trust_safety.contact_cta_body: Get in touch and a coordinator will get back to you. Most messages are answered within one working day.
ga.trust_safety.contact_cta_title: Have a question we haven’t answered?
ga.trust_safety.disputes_intro: Most disagreements can be sorted out between members. If they can’t, we’ll help.
ga.trust_safety.disputes_steps.0: Try first to resolve it directly through messages — most misunderstandings are just that.
ga.trust_safety.disputes_steps.1: If that doesn’t work, contact a coordinator via the Contact page. Coordinators can mediate.
ga.trust_safety.disputes_steps.2: If a member breaches our Community Guidelines, we take action — warnings, mediation, suspension, or removal, depending on severity.
ga.trust_safety.disputes_steps.3: Time-credit disputes (hours logged in error, etc.) are reviewed by an admin who can adjust balances on either side.
ga.trust_safety.heading: Trust & Safety
ga.trust_safety.how_exchanges_intro: Every exchange follows the same simple flow. There is no money involved — just time credits.
ga.trust_safety.how_exchanges_steps.0: Find a member offering what you need, or post a request describing what you’re looking for.
ga.trust_safety.how_exchanges_steps.1: Message them through the platform to agree the details — time, place, and what’s involved.
ga.trust_safety.how_exchanges_steps.2: Complete the exchange. You only meet in person if you both want to.
ga.trust_safety.how_exchanges_steps.3: Log the hours. Both members confirm — that’s the receipt.
ga.trust_safety.how_exchanges_steps.4: Time credits transfer automatically. One hour of help = one time credit, no matter what skill was shared.
ga.trust_safety.insurance_items.0: {{name}} does not provide insurance for exchanges between members. The platform is a connection service — it does not insure, indemnify, or underwrite the work members do for each other.
ga.trust_safety.insurance_items.1: Members are NOT covered by any platform insurance for the work they do for each other. Exchanges are entirely between members and at members’ own risk. See our Terms of Service for the full liability and indemnity position.
ga.trust_safety.insurance_items.2: If you offer professional services (plumbing, electrical, healthcare, legal, financial advice), you’re responsible for your own professional indemnity insurance and any regulatory obligations that apply.
ga.trust_safety.insurance_items.3: Time credits have no monetary value. They can’t be exchanged for cash, sold, or used outside the timebanking community.
ga.trust_safety.meta_description: How exchanges work on {{name}}, what we do to keep the community safe, what we don’t do, and how to report a concern. Plain-English trust and safeguarding.
ga.trust_safety.precautions_intro: You’d apply the same common sense here as you would meeting anyone new through a community group.
ga.trust_safety.precautions_items.0: Meet in a public place the first time, or bring a friend if you’re inviting someone to your home.
ga.trust_safety.precautions_items.1: Read the other member’s reviews and check how long they’ve been on the platform.
ga.trust_safety.precautions_items.3: Trust your instincts. You can say no, end an exchange, or report a concern at any time.
ga.trust_safety.precautions_title: Sensible precautions for a first exchange
ga.trust_safety.responsibilities_items.0: Treat other members with respect (Community Guidelines).
ga.trust_safety.responsibilities_items.1: Be honest about your skills, qualifications, and limitations.
ga.trust_safety.responsibilities_items.2: Show up when you commit, or cancel with reasonable notice.
ga.trust_safety.responsibilities_items.3: Log hours fairly — for the time actually given.
ga.trust_safety.responsibilities_items.4: Use the platform only for community time exchanges, not paid work, sales solicitation, or anything illegal (Acceptable Use Policy).
ga.trust_safety.rights_items.0: Leave at any time and request your data be deleted (GDPR).
ga.trust_safety.rights_items.1: Block other members so they can’t contact you.
ga.trust_safety.rights_items.2: Make your profile invisible to search or browsing.
ga.trust_safety.rights_items.3: Opt out of all optional cookies, analytics, and email notifications.
ga.trust_safety.safeguarding_body: If you’re worried about a member, an exchange, or something you’ve seen on the platform, please tell us. We respond to safeguarding reports within one working day.
ga.trust_safety.safeguarding_step_1: Use the Report button on the relevant listing, message, or profile.
ga.trust_safety.safeguarding_step_2: Or email us via the Contact page — mark the subject as “Safeguarding”.
ga.trust_safety.subtitle: {{name}} connects neighbours so they can share skills using time credits. This page explains how that works in practice — what the platform does, what it doesn’t, and how you can stay safe.
ga.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.0: Verifies every member’s email address at registration; offers optional photo-ID verification with a Verified Member badge for those who want extra trust signals.
ga.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.1: Shows reviews and ratings on member profiles so the community can see who’s been a reliable exchange partner.
ga.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.2: Asks every member to accept our Community Guidelines and Acceptable Use Policy at signup, and to keep to them while they’re here.
ga.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.3: Gives every listing, message, and profile a Report button that goes straight to coordinators.
ga.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.4: Has human coordinators who can mediate, support, and step in when something goes wrong.
ga.trust_safety.what_we_dont_intro: Timebanking is built on neighbours helping neighbours, not on us standing between you. That’s a feature, not a bug — but it does mean we need to be clear about our limits.
ga.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.0: We don’t vet, train, or certify members for specific services. We’re not a recruitment agency.
ga.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.1: We don’t provide insurance coverage for the work members do for each other.
ga.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.2: We don’t supervise exchanges or act as anyone’s employer or service provider.
ga.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.3: We don’t guarantee any specific outcome, level of skill, or punctuality. Reviews are how the community keeps itself accountable.

nl.trust_safety.rights_items.1: Block other members so they can’t contact you.
nl.trust_safety.rights_items.2: Make your profile invisible to search or browsing.
nl.trust_safety.rights_items.3: Opt out of all optional cookies, analytics, and email notifications.
nl.trust_safety.safeguarding_body: If you’re worried about a member, an exchange, or something you’ve seen on the platform, please tell us. We respond to safeguarding reports within one working day.
nl.trust_safety.safeguarding_step_1: Use the Report button on the relevant listing, message, or profile.
nl.trust_safety.safeguarding_step_2: Or email us via the Contact page — mark the subject as “Safeguarding”.
nl.trust_safety.subtitle: {{name}} connects neighbours so they can share skills using time credits. This page explains how that works in practice — what the platform does, what it doesn’t, and how you can stay safe.
nl.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.0: Verifies every member’s email address at registration; offers optional photo-ID verification with a Verified Member badge for those who want extra trust signals.
nl.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.1: Shows reviews and ratings on member profiles so the community can see who’s been a reliable exchange partner.
nl.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.2: Asks every member to accept our Community Guidelines and Acceptable Use Policy at signup, and to keep to them while they’re here.
nl.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.3: Gives every listing, message, and profile a Report button that goes straight to coordinators.
nl.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.4: Has human coordinators who can mediate, support, and step in when something goes wrong.
nl.trust_safety.what_we_dont_intro: Timebanking is built on neighbours helping neighbours, not on us standing between you. That’s a feature, not a bug — but it does mean we need to be clear about our limits.
nl.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.0: We don’t vet, train, or certify members for specific services. We’re not a recruitment agency.
nl.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.1: We don’t provide insurance coverage for the work members do for each other.
nl.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.2: We don’t supervise exchanges or act as anyone’s employer or service provider.
nl.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.3: We don’t guarantee any specific outcome, level of skill, or punctuality. Reviews are how the community keeps itself accountable.

pl.fadp.fadp_banner_body: Under the Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection, we need your explicit consent before using AI-powered features like smart matching and personalisation.
pl.hub.doc_accessibility_desc: Our commitment to making the platform accessible to everyone, including our progress toward WCAG 2.1 AA conformance.
pl.hub.doc_trust_safety_desc: Plain-English guide to how exchanges work, what we do to keep the community safe, what we don’t, and how to report a safeguarding concern.
pl.hub.platform_section_desc: These documents govern the Project NEXUS platform infrastructure and are separate from your community's policies.
pl.page_meta.trust_safety.description: How exchanges work on {{name}}, what we do to keep the community safe, what we don’t do, and how to report a concern. Plain-English trust and safeguarding.
pl.page_meta.trust_safety.title: Trust & Safety
pl.trust_safety.contact_cta_body: Get in touch and a coordinator will get back to you. Most messages are answered within one working day.
pl.trust_safety.contact_cta_title: Have a question we haven’t answered?
pl.trust_safety.disputes_intro: Most disagreements can be sorted out between members. If they can’t, we’ll help.
pl.trust_safety.disputes_steps.0: Try first to resolve it directly through messages — most misunderstandings are just that.
pl.trust_safety.disputes_steps.1: If that doesn’t work, contact a coordinator via the Contact page. Coordinators can mediate.
pl.trust_safety.disputes_steps.2: If a member breaches our Community Guidelines, we take action — warnings, mediation, suspension, or removal, depending on severity.
pl.trust_safety.disputes_steps.3: Time-credit disputes (hours logged in error, etc.) are reviewed by an admin who can adjust balances on either side.
pl.trust_safety.heading: Trust & Safety
pl.trust_safety.how_exchanges_intro: Every exchange follows the same simple flow. There is no money involved — just time credits.
pl.trust_safety.how_exchanges_steps.0: Find a member offering what you need, or post a request describing what you’re looking for.
pl.trust_safety.how_exchanges_steps.1: Message them through the platform to agree the details — time, place, and what’s involved.
pl.trust_safety.how_exchanges_steps.2: Complete the exchange. You only meet in person if you both want to.
pl.trust_safety.how_exchanges_steps.3: Log the hours. Both members confirm — that’s the receipt.
pl.trust_safety.how_exchanges_steps.4: Time credits transfer automatically. One hour of help = one time credit, no matter what skill was shared.
pl.trust_safety.insurance_items.0: {{name}} does not provide insurance for exchanges between members. The platform is a connection service — it does not insure, indemnify, or underwrite the work members do for each other.
pl.trust_safety.insurance_items.1: Members are NOT covered by any platform insurance for the work they do for each other. Exchanges are entirely between members and at members’ own risk. See our Terms of Service for the full liability and indemnity position.
pl.trust_safety.insurance_items.2: If you offer professional services (plumbing, electrical, healthcare, legal, financial advice), you’re responsible for your own professional indemnity insurance and any regulatory obligations that apply.
pl.trust_safety.insurance_items.3: Time credits have no monetary value. They can’t be exchanged for cash, sold, or used outside the timebanking community.
pl.trust_safety.meta_description: How exchanges work on {{name}}, what we do to keep the community safe, what we don’t do, and how to report a concern. Plain-English trust and safeguarding.
pl.trust_safety.precautions_intro: You’d apply the same common sense here as you would meeting anyone new through a community group.
pl.trust_safety.precautions_items.0: Meet in a public place the first time, or bring a friend if you’re inviting someone to your home.
pl.trust_safety.precautions_items.1: Read the other member’s reviews and check how long they’ve been on the platform.
pl.trust_safety.precautions_items.3: Trust your instincts. You can say no, end an exchange, or report a concern at any time.
pl.trust_safety.precautions_title: Sensible precautions for a first exchange
pl.trust_safety.responsibilities_items.0: Treat other members with respect (Community Guidelines).
pl.trust_safety.responsibilities_items.1: Be honest about your skills, qualifications, and limitations.
pl.trust_safety.responsibilities_items.2: Show up when you commit, or cancel with reasonable notice.
pl.trust_safety.responsibilities_items.3: Log hours fairly — for the time actually given.
pl.trust_safety.responsibilities_items.4: Use the platform only for community time exchanges, not paid work, sales solicitation, or anything illegal (Acceptable Use Policy).
pl.trust_safety.rights_items.0: Leave at any time and request your data be deleted (GDPR).
pl.trust_safety.rights_items.1: Block other members so they can’t contact you.
pl.trust_safety.rights_items.2: Make your profile invisible to search or browsing.
pl.trust_safety.rights_items.3: Opt out of all optional cookies, analytics, and email notifications.
pl.trust_safety.safeguarding_body: If you’re worried about a member, an exchange, or something you’ve seen on the platform, please tell us. We respond to safeguarding reports within one working day.
pl.trust_safety.safeguarding_step_1: Use the Report button on the relevant listing, message, or profile.
pl.trust_safety.safeguarding_step_2: Or email us via the Contact page — mark the subject as “Safeguarding”.

pl.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.1: Shows reviews and ratings on member profiles so the community can see who’s been a reliable exchange partner.
pl.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.2: Asks every member to accept our Community Guidelines and Acceptable Use Policy at signup, and to keep to them while they’re here.
pl.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.3: Gives every listing, message, and profile a Report button that goes straight to coordinators.
pl.trust_safety.what_we_do_items.4: Has human coordinators who can mediate, support, and step in when something goes wrong.
pl.trust_safety.what_we_dont_intro: Timebanking is built on neighbours helping neighbours, not on us standing between you. That’s a feature, not a bug — but it does mean we need to be clear about our limits.
pl.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.0: We don’t vet, train, or certify members for specific services. We’re not a recruitment agency.
pl.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.1: We don’t provide insurance coverage for the work members do for each other.
pl.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.2: We don’t supervise exchanges or act as anyone’s employer or service provider.
pl.trust_safety.what_we_dont_items.3: We don’t guarantee any specific outcome, level of skill, or punctuality. Reviews are how the community keeps itself accountable.
